answers: Cinematography = art of visual storytelling through camera. Composition, lighting, movement, and lens choice.
Basic shots: wide (establish), medium (conversation), close-up (emotion), extreme close-up (detail), over-the-shoulder.
Lighting basics: three-point lighting (key, fill, back). Natural light through windows is beautiful and free.
Tips: use the rule of thirds, leading lines, depth of field, and motivated camera movement. Every shot should have a purpose.
